english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Commendo autem vobis Phœben sororem nostram, quæ est in ministerio ecclesiæ, quæ est in Cenchris:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνίστημι δὲ ὑμῖν φοίβην τὴν ἀδελφὴν ἡμῶν οὖσαν διάκονον τῆς ἐκκλησίας τῆς ἐν κεγχρεαῖς
verses.king_james: I commend unto you Phebe our sister, which is a servant of the church which is at Cenchrea:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut eam suscipiatis in Domino digne sanctis: et assistatis ei in quocumque negotio vestri indiguerit: etenim ipsa quoque astitit multis, et mihi ipsi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα αὐτὴν προσδέξησθε ἐν κυρίῳ ἀξίως τῶν ἁγίων καὶ παραστῆτε αὐτῇ ἐν ᾧ ἂν ὑμῶν χρῄζῃ πράγματι καὶ γὰρ αὐτὴ προστάτις πολλῶν ἐγενήθη καὶ αὐτοῦ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Priscam et Aquilam, adjutores meos in Christo Jesu
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε πρίσκιλλαν καὶ ἀκύλαν τοὺς συνεργούς μου ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:

verses.latin_vulgate: (qui pro anima mea suas cervices supposuerunt: quibus non solus ego gratias ago, sed et cunctæ ecclesiæ gentium),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵτινες ὑπὲρ τῆς ψυχῆς μου τὸν ἑαυτῶν τράχηλον ὑπέθηκαν οἷς οὐκ ἐγὼ μόνος εὐχαριστῶ ἀλλὰ καὶ πᾶσαι αἱ ἐκκλησίαι τῶν ἐθνῶν
verses.king_james: Who have for my life laid down their own necks: unto whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.

verses.latin_vulgate: et domesticam ecclesiam eorum. Salutate Epænetum dilectum mihi, qui est primitivus Asiæ in Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὴν κατ᾽ οἶκον αὐτῶν ἐκκλησίαν ἀσπάσασθε ἐπαίνετον τὸν ἀγαπητόν μου ὅς ἐστιν ἀπαρχὴ τῆς ἀχαΐας εἰς χριστόν
verses.king_james: Likewise *greet* the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epaenetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Mariam, quæ multum laboravit in vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε μαρίαμ, ἥτις πολλὰ ἐκοπίασεν εἰς ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: Greet Mary, who bestowed much labour on us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Andronicum et Juniam, cognatos, et concaptivos meos: qui sunt nobiles in Apostolis, qui et ante me fuerunt in Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε ἀνδρόνικον καὶ ἰουνιᾶν τοὺς συγγενεῖς μου καὶ συναιχμαλώτους μου οἵτινές εἰσιν ἐπίσημοι ἐν τοῖς ἀποστόλοις οἳ καὶ πρὸ ἐμοῦ γέγονασιν ἐν χριστῷ
verses.king_james: Salute Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen, and my fellowprisoners, who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Ampliatum dilectissimum mihi in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε ἀμπλίαν τὸν ἀγαπητόν μου ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Greet Amplias my beloved in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Urbanum adjutorem nostrum in Christo Jesu, et Stachyn dilectum meum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε οὐρβανὸν τὸν συνεργὸν ἡμῶν ἐν χριστῷ καὶ στάχυν τὸν ἀγαπητόν μου
verses.king_james: Salute Urbane, our helper in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Apellen probum in Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε ἀπελλῆν τὸν δόκιμον ἐν χριστῷ ἀσπάσασθε τοὺς ἐκ τῶν ἀριστοβούλου
verses.king_james: Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus' *household*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate eos qui sunt ex Aristoboli domo. Salutate Herodionem cognatum meum. Salutate eos qui sunt ex Narcisi domo, qui sunt in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε ἡροδίωνα τὸν συγγενῆ μου ἀσπάσασθε τοὺς ἐκ τῶν ναρκίσσου τοὺς ὄντας ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the *household* of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Tryphænam et Tryphosam, quæ laborant in Domino. Salutate Persidem carissimam, quæ multum laboravit in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε τρύφαιναν καὶ τρυφῶσαν τὰς κοπιώσας ἐν κυρίῳ ἀσπάσασθε περσίδα τὴν ἀγαπητήν ἥτις πολλὰ ἐκοπίασεν ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloved Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Rufum electum in Domino, et matrem ejus, et meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε Ῥοῦφον τὸν ἐκλεκτὸν ἐν κυρίῳ καὶ τὴν μητέρα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Asyncritum, Phlegontem, Hermam, Patrobam, Hermen, et qui cum eis sunt, fratres.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε ἀσύγκριτον φλέγοντα ἑρμᾶν πατροβᾶν ἑρμῆν καὶ τοὺς σὺν αὐτοῖς ἀδελφούς
verses.king_james: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate Philologum et Juliam, Nereum, et sororem ejus, et Olympiadem, et omnes qui cum eis sunt, sanctos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε φιλόλογον καὶ ἰουλίαν νηρέα καὶ τὴν ἀδελφὴν αὐτοῦ καὶ ὀλυμπᾶν καὶ τοὺς σὺν αὐτοῖς πάντας ἁγίους
verses.king_james: Salute Philologus, and Julia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate invicem in osculo sancto. Salutant vos omnes ecclesiæ Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε ἀλλήλους ἐν φιλήματι ἁγίῳ ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς αἱ ἐκκλησίαι τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Salute one another with an holy kiss. The churches of Christ salute you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rogo autem vos fratres, ut observetis eos qui dissensiones et offendicula, præter doctrinam, quam vos didicistis, faciunt, et declinate ab illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς ἀδελφοί σκοπεῖν τοὺς τὰς διχοστασίας καὶ τὰ σκάνδαλα παρὰ τὴν διδαχὴν ἣν ὑμεῖς ἐμάθετε ποιοῦντας καὶ ἐκκλίνατε ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Now I beseech you, brethren, mark them which cause divisions and offences contrary to the doctrine which ye have learned; and avoid them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hujuscemodi enim Christo Domino nostro non serviunt, sed suo ventri: et per dulces sermones et benedictiones seducunt corda innocentium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ γὰρ τοιοῦτοι τῷ κυρίῳ ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστῷ οὐ δουλεύουσιν ἀλλὰ τῇ ἑαυτῶν κοιλίᾳ καὶ διὰ τῆς χρηστολογίας καὶ εὐλογίας ἐξαπατῶσιν τὰς καρδίας τῶν ἀκάκων
verses.king_james: For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and fair speeches deceive the hearts of the simple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vestra enim obedientia in omnem locum divulgata est. Gaudeo igitur in vobis. Sed volo vos sapientes esse in bono, et simplices in malo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γὰρ ὑμῶν ὑπακοὴ εἰς πάντας ἀφίκετο χαίρω οὖν τὸ ἐφ᾽ ὑμῖν θέλω δὲ ὑμᾶς σοφοὺς μέν εἶναι εἰς τὸ ἀγαθόν ἀκεραίους δὲ εἰς τὸ κακόν
verses.king_james: For your obedience is come abroad unto all *men*. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you wise unto that which is good, and simple concerning evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem pacis conterat Satanam sub pedibus vestris velociter. Gratia Domini nostri Jesu Christi vobiscum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς εἰρήνης συντρίψει τὸν σατανᾶν ὑπὸ τοὺς πόδας ὑμῶν ἐν τάχει ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And the God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ *be* with you. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutat vos Timotheus adjutor meus, et Lucius, et Jason, et Sosipater cognati mei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζονταί ὑμᾶς τιμόθεος ὁ συνεργός μου καὶ λούκιος καὶ ἰάσων καὶ σωσίπατρος οἱ συγγενεῖς μου
verses.king_james: Timotheus my workfellow, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen, salute you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Saluto vos ego Tertius, qui scripsi epistolam, in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζομαι ὑμᾶς ἐγὼ τέρτιος ὁ γράψας τὴν ἐπιστολὴν ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: I Tertius, who wrote *this* epistle, salute you in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutat vos Cajus hospes meus, et universa ecclesia. Salutat vos Erastus arcarius civitatis, et Quartus, frater.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς γάϊος ὁ ξένος μου καὶ τῆς ἐκκλησίας ὅλης ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς ἕραστος ὁ οἰκονόμος τῆς πόλεως καὶ κούαρτος ὁ ἀδελφός
verses.king_james: Gaius mine host, and of the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus the chamberlain of the city saluteth you, and Quartus a brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia Domini nostri Jesu Christi cum omnibus vobis. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἀμήν
verses.king_james: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ *be* with you all. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ei autem, qui potens est vos confirmare juxta Evangelium meum, et pr��dicationem Jesu Christi, secundum revelationem mysterii temporibus æternis taciti
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ δὲ δυναμένῳ ὑμᾶς στηρίξαι κατὰ τὸ εὐαγγέλιόν μου καὶ τὸ κήρυγμα ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ κατὰ ἀποκάλυψιν μυστηρίου χρόνοις αἰωνίοις σεσιγημένου
verses.king_james: Now to him that is of power to stablish you according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery, which was kept secret since the world began,

verses.latin_vulgate: (quod nunc patefactum est per Scripturas prophetarum secundum præceptum æterni Dei, ad obeditionem fidei), in cunctis gentibus cogniti,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φανερωθέντος δὲ νῦν διά τε γραφῶν προφητικῶν κατ᾽ ἐπιταγὴν τοῦ αἰωνίου θεοῦ εἰς ὑπακοὴν πίστεως εἰς πάντα τὰ ἔθνη γνωρισθέντος
verses.king_james: But now is made manifest, and by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the everlasting God, made known to all nations for the obedience of faith:

verses.latin_vulgate: soli sapienti Deo, per Jesum Christum, cui honor et gloria in sæcula sæculorum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μόνῳ σοφῷ θεῷ διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἶῶνας ἆμήν πρός Ῥωμαίους ἐγράφη ἆπό κορίνθου διὰ φοίβης τῆς διακόνου τῆς ἕν κεγχρεαῖς ἐκκλησίας
verses.king_james: To God only wise, *be* glory through Jesus Christ for ever. Amen.
